methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from various quarries in the Polish Jura, covering specific Jurassic geological periods, for subsequent foraminiferal analysis. Samples were collected from the Wrzosowa, Kawodrza, Stare Gliny, Ogrodzieniec, Gnaszyn, Bolęcin, Czatkowice, Gorenice, and Podłęże quarries, exposing strata from the Morrisi Zone (middle Bathonian) to the Bifurcatus Zone (upper Oxfordian).

steps.description: Identify sample locations (quarries) and their geological context (ammonite zones, strata types).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify geological sample locations and context based on literature and field knowledge.
inputs: Previous literature on Polish Jura sections
outputs: Associated geological zones and strata types

steps.description: Collect a specified number of samples from each identified location and stratigraphical division, totaling 43 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples from specified geological locations and strata.

steps.description: Record location details, including coordinates where available (e.g., Ogrodzieniec, Wrzosowa Quarry).
steps.searchable_summary: Record sample location details and coordinates.
outputs: Sample metadata (location, coordinates, number of samples per location/zone)

methods.method_name: Sediment Sample Preparation for Foraminifera Extraction
methods.description: Laboratory procedure to disaggregate clay-rich sediment samples and isolate foraminifera specimens for microscopic examination. This involves chemical disintegration, washing, sieving, and optional liquid nitrogen treatment.

steps.description: Select clay-rich samples for processing, as they provide excellent conditions for fossil preservation and isolation.
steps.searchable_summary: Select sediment samples with high clay content suitable for disaggregation.

steps.description: Disintegrate selected samples using Glauber's salt (Na2SO4 × 10H2O) or detergent.
steps.searchable_summary: Disintegrate sediment samples using chemical agents like Glauber's salt or detergent.

steps.description: Wash the disintegrated material in an ultrasonic cleaner.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean disintegrated sediment material using an ultrasonic bath.

steps.description: Sieve the washed material using two sieves: 0.071 mm (lower) and 0.6 mm (upper).
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve processed sediment material using specified mesh sizes.
outputs: Sieved residue (material between 0.071 mm and 0.6 mm)

steps.description: Treat samples that required additional cleaning with liquid nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply liquid nitrogen treatment for further cleaning of sieved residue.

methods.description: Procedure for identifying, studying, and imaging isolated foraminifera specimens using stereoscopic and scanning electron microscopy to obtain detailed morphological and taxonomic data.

steps.description: Manually pick foraminifera from the residue.
steps.searchable_summary: Manually isolate foraminifera specimens from the sieved sediment residue.

steps.description: Study the picked foraminifera with the use of a Nikon SMZ18 stereoscopic microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine foraminifera morphology and taxonomy using a stereoscopic microscope.

steps.description: Generate taxonomic observations and images using a Zeiss Sigma VP scanning electron microscope at the Faculty of Geology, University of Warsaw.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture high-resolution images and generate taxonomic data using a scanning electron microscope (SEM).
inputs: Zeiss Sigma VP scanning electron microscope (SEM)
